content.full_text:
Re:
Fern PolrrrcAl- PRACrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento. CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
November 17,2009
Sandra Hamlat
Associate Park and Recreation Specialist
Baldwin Hills Conservancy
5120 West Goldleaf Circle, Suite 290
Los Angeles, CA 90056
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-09-236
Dear Ms. Hamlat:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the honorarium
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Please note that our advice is based
solely on the provisions of the Act. We offer no opinion on the application of any other
bodies of law such as Govemment Code Section 19990, which allows each state agency
to develop a statement of incompatible activities designating specific enterprises or
employment "clearly inconsistent, incompatible, in conflict with, or inimical to" the
duties of the agency's officials and employees. We encourage you to consult with your
agency's counsel and review your statement of incompatible activities to ensure that your
activities do not violate conflict-of-interest laws outside the Act and are not otherwise
incompatible with your responsibilities to the agency.
QrrEsTroN
Is a payment for $500 for participating in meetings of a student advisory
committee regarding a student's thesis project, and providing guidance to the student
regarding the project, a prohibited gift or honorarium under the Act?
CONCLUSION
Because your position with the Baldwin Hills Conservancy is not designated in
the agency's conflict-of-interest code, a payment of $500 for your participation in
I The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All
statutory ret'erences are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
PoliticalPractices Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through 18997 of Title 2 of rhe California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of
Regulations. unless otherwise indicated.
=!"


File No. A-09-236
Page No. 2
meetings of a student's advisory committee is not reportable as income or a gift.
Accordingly, the payment is not a prohibited gift or honorarium under the Act.
FACTS
You are an Associate Park and Recreation Specialist at the Baldwin Hills
Conservancy, a position which is not designated in the agency's conflict-of-interest
code." The Baldwin Hills Conservancy, a state agency, was established by the state
legislature in2002. The mission of the agency is to acquire open space and manage
public lands within the Baldwin Hills area. In addition, the agency provides recreation,
restoration, and protection of wildlife habitat within the territory for the public's
enjoyment and educational experience.
Recently, you were asked to sit on a graduate student's advisory committee, in the
Architecture Department at the University of Southern California (the "University"), for a
thesis project analyzing water systems in the Baldwin Hills area and recommending a
restoration strategy. As a member of the advisory committee, you will attend periodic
meetings with the student and committee to provide guidance on the student's project.
The University has offered you an "honorarium" of $500 for participating in these
meetings and providing guidance on the project.
AI\ALYSIS
The Act imposes different obligations on public officials regarding the receipt of
income, gifts, and honoraria. When an agency's conflict-of-interest code requires a
designated employee to report income and gifts from specified persons on the official's
statement of economic interests, the employee must report income from each person
when it amounts to $500 or more, and gifts from each person when they amount to $50 or
more, during the applicable reporting period. (Sections 87207(a)(l) and 87302(b).)
Additionally, a designated employee may not accept gifts from a single source in any
calendar year in excess of the gift limit if the official is required to report the receipt of
gifts from that source on his or her statement of economic interests. (Section S9503(c).)
The current gift limit is $420. (Regulation 18940.2)
A designated employee is also prohibited from accepting "an honorarium from
any source if the member or employee would be required to report the receipt of income
or gifts from that source on his or her statement of economic interests." (Section
89502(c).) Section 89501 defines the term "honorarium," in pertinent part, as follows:
"(a) For purposes of this chapter, 'honorarium' means, except as
provided in subdivision (b), any payment made in consideration for any
2 You confirmed that your position was not designated in Baldwin Hills Conservancy's conflict-
of-interest code in a telephone conversation on November 9,2009.


File No. A-09-236
Page No. 3
speech given, article published, or attendance at any public or private
conference, convention, meeting, social event, meal, or like gathering."
However, a review of the conflict-of-interest code for the Baldwin Hills
Conservancy reveals that your position is not a designated position, and you are not
required to report income or gifts from any source. Because the payment of $500 from
the University for your participation in meetings of a student's advisory committee is not
reportable income or a reportable gift, the payment is not a prohibited gift or honorarium
under the Act.3
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division
BGLjgl
3 Notwithstanding the conclusion that the Act does not prohibit the payment from the University,
the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply to all public officials, even when the official has not been
designated in his or her agency's conflict-of-interest code. Under these provisions, a public official who
receives or is promised income aggregating to $500, or a gift or gifts aggregating to $420, from a single
source within l2 months preceding a governmental decision must disqualify ninrsetf or herself from the
decision if the decision will have a reasonably foreseeable material effect on the source. (Section g700, et
se4'; Regulations 18703.3 and 18703.4.) Therefore, if you accept the $500 payment, you have received
income or a gift fiom the University and may be prohibited from making, pirticipating in making, or
influencing governmental decisions affecting the University. If you need additional aisistance regarding
any particular decision you may take part in affecting the University, you should seek further advice
providing all relevant facts.
